learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Sorting, Two Pointers, Loops, Time Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine selecting four different products whose total price must exactly match a gift card balance. Instead of checking every possible group of four products, sorting allows you to efficiently adjust the total using two pointers.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine selecting four different products whose total price must exactly match a gift card balance. Instead of checking every possible group of four products, sorting allows you to efficiently adjust the total using two pointers.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use this pattern whenever you need to find unique quadruplets or combinations of four numbers that satisfy a target sum.
learning.signals: Whenever a problem asks for unique combinations of four numbers summing to a target, think of Sorting followed by Two Pointers.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: Given an integer array nums of n integers, return all unique quadruplets [nums[a], nums[b], nums[c], nums[d]] such that a, b, c, and d are distinct indices, nums[a] + nums[b] + nums[c] + nums[d] == target, and no duplicate quadruplets are included.

examples.explanation: These are the only unique quadruplets whose sum equals 0.

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: Check every possible combination of four elements and collect valid unique quadruplets.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: After sorting, fix the first two numbers. The remaining two numbers can be found efficiently using the Two Pointer technique, similar to the Three Sum problem.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: After fixing two numbers, the remaining problem becomes Two Sum on a sorted array.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever a problem asks for unique combinations of four numbers summing to a target, think of Sorting followed by Two Pointers.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Sorting enables the Two Pointer technique, reducing the search for the last two numbers from O(n²) to O(n). Duplicate skipping guarantees unique quadruplets.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever a problem asks for unique combinations of four numbers summing to a target, think of Sorting followed by Two Pointers.
commonWrongPaths: Using int instead of long for large sums.
Moving only one pointer after finding a quadruplet.

hints: Use Two Pointers for the remaining pair.
commonMisconceptions: Using int instead of long for large sums.
Moving only one pointer after finding a quadruplet.

pattern.name: Sorting + Two Pointers
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever a problem asks for unique combinations of four numbers summing to a target, think of Sorting followed by Two Pointers.

approaches.title: Four Nested Loops
approaches.idea: Check every possible combination of four elements and collect valid unique quadruplets.
algorithm: Loop j from i+1 to n-3.
Loop k from j+1 to n-2.
Loop l from k+1 to n-1.
If sum equals target, store unique quadruplet.
approaches.dryRun:
nums=[1,0,-1,0]
Check every group of four elements.

code.java: Brute force omitted because it requires four nested loops.

approaches.title: HashSet for Third and Fourth Elements
approaches.idea: Fix the first two numbers and use a HashSet to find the remaining two numbers while avoiding duplicates.
approaches.dryRun:
Fix -2 and -1.
Find remaining pair using HashSet.

approaches.title: Sorting + Two Pointers
approaches.idea: Sort the array. Fix the first two numbers using nested loops. Then use two pointers to find the remaining two numbers whose sum completes the target. Skip duplicate values to avoid repeated quadruplets.
approaches.intuition: Sorting enables the Two Pointer technique, reducing the search for the last two numbers from O(n²) to O(n). Duplicate skipping guarantees unique quadruplets.
algorithm: Loop j from i+1 to n-3.
Initialize left = j+1 and right = n-1.
If total equals target, store quadruplet.
Move both pointers while skipping duplicates.
If total is smaller, move left.
approaches.dryRun:
nums=[1,0,-1,0,-2,2]
Sorted=[-2,-1,0,0,1,2]
Fix -2,-1
left=0 right=2
Sum=-1
Move left
Sum=0 ✓
Store [-2,-1,1,2]

code.cpp: Uses sorting, two nested loops, and two pointers with duplicate skipping.
code.javascript: Uses sorting with nested loops and two pointers.

visualization.description: Fix two numbers and use Two Pointers for the remaining two.

interviewFollowUps: How does this relate to Three Sum?
Can this be generalized to K Sum?
How do you avoid duplicate quadruplets?
Why should long be used for the sum?
interviewTips: Mention the similarity with Three Sum.
Always sort before using Two Pointers.
Use long to prevent integer overflow.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: After fixing two numbers, the remaining problem becomes Two Sum on a sorted array.

commonMistakes: Using int instead of long for large sums.
Moving only one pointer after finding a quadruplet.
